tier.busy: You can't switch modes while a job is running

recipe.label: Steps
recipe.backToStart: Back to ① Put data in (start over)

s1.title: Add measurement data
s1.dropTitle: Drop your measurement data files here
s1.dropFormats: Excel, CSV, instrument exports (.txt / .dat), Word / PDF
s1.privacy: Once dropped, everything proceeds automatically. Nothing is visible to anyone until you publish.
s1.unsupported: This file format is not supported yet. Please drop CSV, instrument exports, Word or PDF files.
s1.mixed: These files are of different kinds. Please add tables and documents separately.
s1.documentNote: Documents (Word / PDF / XML) can be taken in as they are — no AI design needed. Use the form below.
s1.aiNotReady: The AI is not set up yet. Once an administrator configures an AI key on the server, this screen works as is. To use your own key, enter it in Settings.
s1.resumeNote: You have unfinished work. Drop the same files again to resume from the review screen.

s2.title: Check the reading
s2.lead: Here is how your files were read. Please answer just two things only you would know.
s2.leadNoQuestions: Here is how your files were read. Check the content and continue.
s2.fileCard: No content preview for this file (it will be read as is).
s2.q1: Before the table starts, there were {{count}} lines that look like sample information. Should this be recorded together with each measurement?
s2.q2: Is this "{{column}}" a serial number used only inside this file? Or does the same number also appear in other files or papers?
s2.needAnswers: Answer the questions above to continue.

s3.jobLabel: The AI is reading your data (1–3 min)
s3.closeNote: You can close this screen — the work continues.

s4.gateHint: Please confirm just one thing: what one row of this table represents.
s4.continuing: Writing down what each column means…
s4.needFiles: The files are missing. Please drop them again on the first screen.

s5.title: Reading the column meanings and loading your draft
s5.meanings: Wrote down what each column means
s5.quality: Auto-fixed the quality checks (8 items — no garbled text, no overlapping IDs, and more)
s5.closeNote: You can close this screen if you like.

stop.materialize: Saving the design hit an error
stop.attach: Saving your files hit an error
stop.ingest: Loading the draft hit an error
stop.design: Some problems in the design still need attention
stop.designBody: These could not be fixed automatically. Ask the AI to fix them with the button below, or review them in detail mode.
stop.filesBody: Drop the same files here again to continue where you left off.
stop.interruptedBody: Nothing partial was kept. "Try again" safely resumes from the same point.
stop.restartConfirm: This clears the files you added and everything so far. Start over from the beginning?

plain.tokenTitle: You don't have permission to save
plain.tokenBody: Enter the access code you received from your administrator in Settings.
plain.notFoundTitle: Your saved data was not found
plain.notFoundBody: It may have been deleted. Please start over from the beginning.
plain.timeoutTitle: The AI didn't respond in time
plain.timeoutBody: In Settings, switch to a faster-responding model such as Claude and try again.
plain.designTitle: Part of the design still can't be loaded
plain.designBody: The AI's design still has a part that can't be loaded as-is. Try "Have the AI fix this", or review it in detail mode.
plain.serverBody: Please wait a moment and try again.
plain.genericBody: Please try again. If it persists, check the technical details below or open detail mode.

fix.progress: The AI is fixing the design (attempt {{n}}, 1–3 min)
fix.failed: The AI fix didn't go through: {{message}}

s6.title: Check (2/2) — column meanings
s6.lead: Please check what the AI read each column to mean. The words you confirm here are used for search and citation.
s6.missingMeaning: No meaning could be read for this column. If it matters, tell us below.
s6.noteLabel: Anything that looks off? One line is enough
s6.notePlaceholder: e.g. "ZT" means the dimensionless figure of merit / temperatures are in K
s6.reflect: Have the AI apply it and rebuild
s6.reflecting: The AI is applying your note to the design (1–3 min)
s6.refineWrap: While reviewing the column meanings, the user pointed this out. Follow it and fix the affected columns' meaning (label) and unit (unit) — and the design itself if needed: {{note}}

s7.title: Try it — we asked your data a few questions
s7.lead: Registered. To double-check, a few questions were asked automatically.
s7.failed: The trial queries could not run. Wait a moment and press "Try again", or continue to publish as is.
s7.qCountMany: What kinds of data are there, and how many of each?
s7.qCountAny: How many records are in there?
s7.cite: → Open where this number comes from (its ID)
s7.traceNote: Every number can be traced back to the row it came from.
s7.back: Something is off — back to column meanings

s8.title: Publish
s8.needName: Give it a name to show in the public list, and you can publish.
s8.words: Used {{reuse}} common standard words as they are, and made {{added}} new words for this data.
s8.wordsNew: New words for this data only
s8.promise: If you spot a mistake, you can withdraw it from citation at any time (the data and IDs remain, so links to already-cited numbers never break).
s8.publishNote: Once published, answers to questions can cite this data with its source.

s9.title: Published
s9.askHint: Click one and the Ask screen opens with the question filled in.
s9.append: Add new measurements (the same instrument files as they are)
s9.replace: Replace everything (search keeps working during the swap)

job.resumedFailed: Could not resume the interrupted work: {{message}}
